name: rust-log-monitor
description: Monitor Rust application logs (tracing-appender output) using an LLM to detect errors, panics, and stack traces in real-time. Returns immediately with an operation ID; push alerts are delivered as MCP progress notifications whenever the LLM detects an issue. Use `cancel` with the operation ID to stop monitoring.

livelog.detection_prompt: Look for ERROR or WARN level lines, thread panics ('thread .* panicked at'), unwrap failures ('called `Option::unwrap()` on a `None` value' or 'called `Result::unwrap()` on an `Err` value'), stack traces (lines starting with whitespace followed by 'at' or containing '::' backtraces), SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, or any log line indicating a real problem rather than normal operation.

custom.usage: Call this tool to start live monitoring of Rust tracing-appender log output. The tool watches `./log/ahma_mcp.log` using `tail -F` (follows through log rotation). Returns an operation ID immediately; MCP progress notifications appear when the LLM detects errors or panics. Call `cancel` with the operation ID when finished.
custom.prerequisites: The Rust application must write logs via tracing-appender to `./log/`. Set RUST_LOG to control log verbosity (e.g. RUST_LOG=debug). Ollama must be running locally on port 11434 with the `llama3.2` model available (`ollama pull llama3.2`).
custom.log_path: Default watches `./log/ahma_mcp.log`. For other Rust projects, edit `source_args` to point to your tracing-appender log file.
